Class ServerNetworkHandler

java.lang.Object
java.rmi.server.RemoteObject
java.rmi.server.RemoteServer
java.rmi.server.UnicastRemoteObject
it.polimi.ingsw.am25.server.webLayer.RMI.ServerNetworkHandler
All Implemented Interfaces:
ServerRemoteInterface, Serializable, Remote
public class ServerNetworkHandler extends UnicastRemoteObject implements ServerRemoteInterface
RMI server-side entry point for all Mesos client actions. Manages the game lobby, registers client stubs, and delegates game logic to the Controller. Also used as the target for Socket clients via SocketClientHandler.
See Also:
  • Serialized Form

    Fields inherited from class RemoteObject

    ref

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    ServerNetworkHandler()
    Initializes the RMI network handler and exports it as a remote object, making it reachable by Mesos clients via the RMI registry.

    Method
    Description
    void
    addPlayer(PlayerDTO playerDTO, ClientRemoteInterface clientRemoteInterface)
    Adds a player to the existing Mesos lobby and registers their RMI stub.

    Retrieves leaderboards from the database for all player-count configurations from 2 up to the given number, and sends them to the requesting client via ClientRemoteInterface.sendRank(Map).

    Called by SocketClientHandler when a socket client's stream drops.

    Allows a player to rejoin a game that is being loaded, after the first player has invoked loadGame(PlayerDTO, ClientRemoteInterface).

    Starts loading a saved game from disk.

    Central disconnection handler.

    Receives a heartbeat ping from a client and resets that player's missed-ping counter.

    Places the player's totem on the offer tile at the given board position during the placing phase.

    Signals that the player voluntarily skips their draw action for this turn, without selecting any card from the market.
    void

    Lets the player pick a card from the previous-round (bottom) market row during the resolve-action phase.
    void

    Lets the player pick a card from the current-round (top) market row during the resolve-action phase.
    void

    Selects the bonus card granted by the draw-one-more mechanic (triggered after the server calls askExtraDraw on the client).

    Called when the player declines the extra draw granted by the draw-one-more building effect.

  • Constructor Details

    • ServerNetworkHandler

      public ServerNetworkHandler() throws RemoteException
      Initializes the RMI network handler and exports it as a remote object, making it reachable by Mesos clients via the RMI registry.
      Throws:
      RemoteException - if the RMI export fails.

  • Method Details

    • createGame

      public void createGame(PlayerDTO playerHost, int playerNumber, ClientRemoteInterface clientRemoteInterface) throws RemoteException, IllegalStateException
      Creates a new Mesos game lobby hosted by the given player. Registers the host's RMI stub so the server can push game events (board updates, phase changes, market refreshes, etc.) back to them during the game.
      Specified by:
      createGame in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      playerHost - the host's data (nickname and totem color).
      playerNumber - the number of players required to start the game (2–5).
      clientRemoteInterface - the host's RMI stub, saved to notify them of game events.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if a lobby is already open on this server.
      RemoteException - if the RMI call fails.

    • addPlayer

      public void addPlayer(PlayerDTO playerDTO, ClientRemoteInterface clientRemoteInterface) throws RemoteException, GameFullException, GameReadyToStartException, NameOrColorAlreadyTakenException
      Adds a player to the existing Mesos lobby and registers their RMI stub. If this player fills the last required slot, the game starts automatically.
      Specified by:
      addPlayer in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      playerDTO - the joining player's data (nickname and totem color).
      clientRemoteInterface - the player's RMI stub, saved to notify them of game events.
      Throws:
      GameFullException - if no lobby exists yet (no host has called createGame(PlayerDTO, int, ClientRemoteInterface)).
      GameStartedException - if the game is already running.
      NameOrColorAlreadyTakenException - if the chosen nickname or totem color is already taken by another player.
      RemoteException - if the RMI call fails.
      GameReadyToStartException - if this player fills the last slot (game will start).

    • placingPlayer

      public void placingPlayer(PlayerDTO playerToPlace, int position) throws RemoteException, IndexOutOfBoundsException, TileOccupiedException
      Places the player's totem on the offer tile at the given board position during the placing phase. The chosen tile determines how many draws from the top and bottom market rows the player will have in the resolve-action phase.
      Specified by:
      placingPlayer in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      playerToPlace - the player placing their totem.
      position - the index of the target offer tile on the board.
      Throws:
      TileOccupiedException - if another player's totem is already on that tile.
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if position is out of range.
      RemoteException - if the RMI call fails.

    • selectCardFromTopList

      public void selectCardFromTopList(PlayerDTO player, CARD_TYPE cardType, int position) throws RemoteException, IndexOutOfBoundsException, NotEnoughFoodException, NotSelectableCardException, EmptyMarketException
      Lets the player pick a card from the current-round (top) market row during the resolve-action phase. Tribe member cards (Hunter, Gatherer, Builder, etc.) are added to the player's tribe for free; building cards cost food equal to their price.
      Specified by:
      selectCardFromTopList in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      player - the player making the selection.
      cardType - the type of card being selected (tribe member or CARD_TYPE.BUILDING).
      position - the index of the card in the top row.
      Throws:
      NotEnoughFoodException - if the player lacks the food to buy a building.
      NotSelectableCardException - if the card at that position is an event card and cannot be picked.
      EmptyMarketException - if the top row has no selectable cards.
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if position is out of range.
      RemoteException - if the RMI call fails.

    • selectCardFromBottomList

      public void selectCardFromBottomList(PlayerDTO player, CARD_TYPE cardType, int position) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException, NotEnoughFoodException, NotSelectableCardException, EmptyMarketException, RemoteException
      Lets the player pick a card from the previous-round (bottom) market row during the resolve-action phase.
      Specified by:
      selectCardFromBottomList in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      player - the player making the selection.
      cardType - the type of card being selected (tribe member or CARD_TYPE.BUILDING).
      position - the index of the card in the bottom row.
      Throws:
      NotEnoughFoodException - if the player lacks the food to buy a building.
      NotSelectableCardException - if the card at that position is an event card and cannot be picked.
      EmptyMarketException - if the bottom row has no selectable cards.
      IndexOutOfBoundsException - if position is out of range.
      RemoteException - if the RMI call fails.

    • playerDoNothing

      public void playerDoNothing(PlayerDTO playerDTO) throws Exception
      Signals that the player voluntarily skips their draw action for this turn, without selecting any card from the market.
      Specified by:
      playerDoNothing in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      playerDTO - the player who is skipping their action.
      Throws:
      Exception - if an unexpected error occurs.

    • selectExtraCard

      public void selectExtraCard(PlayerDTO player, CARD_TYPE cardType, int position) throws RemoteException
      Selects the bonus card granted by the draw-one-more mechanic (triggered after the server calls askExtraDraw on the client). The player picks one additional card from either market row on top of their normal action. Any checked exception from the controller is rethrown as a RemoteException.
      Specified by:
      selectExtraCard in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      player - the player claiming the extra card.
      cardType - the type of card being selected (tribe member or CARD_TYPE.BUILDING).
      position - the index of the card in the relevant market row.
      Throws:
      RemoteException - if the RMI call fails.

    • skipExtraDraw

      public void skipExtraDraw(PlayerDTO player) throws RemoteException
      Called when the player declines the extra draw granted by the draw-one-more building effect. The server does not block waiting for the player's response — askExtraDraw is fire-and-forget — so this method is a no-op at the controller level. It exists so the client has a symmetric call to make regardless of whether they pick a card or not.
      Specified by:
      skipExtraDraw in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      player - the player declining the extra draw.
      Throws:
      RemoteException - if the RMI call fails.

    • askForRank

      public void askForRank(String playerNumber, ClientRemoteInterface clientRemoteInterface) throws RemoteException
      Retrieves leaderboards from the database for all player-count configurations from 2 up to the given number, and sends them to the requesting client via ClientRemoteInterface.sendRank(Map). If the database is unreachable for a given size, the corresponding entry is replaced with an error message.
      Specified by:
      askForRank in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      playerNumber - the maximum number of players for which to load the leaderboard, provided as a string and converted internally to an integer.
      clientRemoteInterface - the RMI stub (or Socket proxy) of the client to send the leaderboards to.
      Throws:
      RemoteException - if an RMI communication error occurs.

    • ping

      public void ping(PlayerDTO player) throws RemoteException
      Receives a heartbeat ping from a client and resets that player's missed-ping counter. Uses a ConcurrentHashMap lookup so it does not need the global lock.
      Specified by:
      ping in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      player - the player sending the ping (identified by nickname).
      Throws:
      RemoteException - if the RMI call fails.

    • handleSocketClientDisconnection

      public void handleSocketClientDisconnection(ClientRemoteInterface proxy)
      Called by SocketClientHandler when a socket client's stream drops. Finds the virtual view matching the given proxy and triggers disconnection handling.
      Parameters:
      proxy - the ClientRemoteInterface proxy whose socket closed.

    • notifyPlayerDisconnected

      public void notifyPlayerDisconnected(String nickname)
      Central disconnection handler. Marks the view as disconnected, broadcasts a playerDisconnected notification to all clients, then tells the controller to update the game model and advance the turn if needed.
      Parameters:
      nickname - the disconnected player's nickname.

    • loadGame

      public void loadGame(PlayerDTO playerDTO, ClientRemoteInterface clientRemoteInterface) throws RemoteException, GameAlreadyLoadedException, NoGameToLoadException
      Starts loading a saved game from disk. The first player to invoke this method becomes the "loader": the controller is initialised with the persisted data and their view is registered while waiting for the other players to reconnect via joinGameLoaded(PlayerDTO, ClientRemoteInterface).
      Specified by:
      loadGame in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      playerDTO - the data of the player requesting the load (nickname and totem colour).
      clientRemoteInterface - the RMI stub (or Socket proxy) of the loading client.
      Throws:
      RemoteException - if an RMI communication error occurs.
      GameAlreadyLoadedException - if an active controller already exists (game already in progress or loaded).
      NoGameToLoadException - if no saved game exists to load.

    • joinGameLoaded

      public void joinGameLoaded(PlayerDTO playerDTO, ClientRemoteInterface clientRemoteInterface) throws RemoteException, IllegalStateException, GameReadyToStartException
      Allows a player to rejoin a game that is being loaded, after the first player has invoked loadGame(PlayerDTO, ClientRemoteInterface). When all expected players have reconnected, the game resumes automatically.
      Specified by:
      joinGameLoaded in interface ServerRemoteInterface
      Parameters:
      playerDTO - the data of the reconnecting player (nickname and totem colour).
      clientRemoteInterface - the RMI stub (or Socket proxy) of the reconnecting client.
      Throws:
      RemoteException - if an RMI communication error occurs, or if the nickname is not present in the saved game.
      IllegalStateException - if no game is currently being loaded.
      GameReadyToStartException - (not propagated externally) when the last player reconnects and the game is started internally.
